changes_made: Changed k8s_rbac_temporary_access_restriction to k8s_rbac_temporary_access_time_bound for clarity
Changed k8s_rbac_emergency_access_audit to k8s_rbac_emergency_access_logging for specificity
review_notes: The functions are applicable to Kubernetes as they address RBAC management for temporary and emergency accounts, aligning with the FedRAMP AC-2 (2) control. The original function names were somewhat vague, so adjustments were made for clarity and specificity. The revised names better reflect the actions being checked and align with Kubernetes security practices.
naming_improvements: Use 'time_bound' instead of 'restriction' to specify the nature of temporary access
Use 'logging' instead of 'audit' for clarity in emergency access monitoring
title: Account Management | Automated Temporary and Emergency Account Management
step1_reasoning: The control AC-2 (2) focuses on managing temporary and emergency accounts, which can be mapped to Kubernetes by ensuring that temporary access is restricted and audited. This involves managing RBAC roles and bindings to ensure that service accounts or user accounts with elevated privileges are monitored and have time-bound access. Additionally, auditing emergency access and enforcing expiry on service accounts aligns with the control's intent.
